message:
feat(education): Add PhageLifecycle educational module

Comprehensive educational module covering phage lifecycle:
- Lytic cycle stages with timing (attach, inject, takeover, replicate, assemble, lyse)
- Lysogenic cycle stages (integration, dormancy, induction)
- Lytic vs lysogenic comparison table
- Lambda decision circuit diagram (cI vs Cro)
- Key vocabulary and concepts
- Integrates InfectionCycleVisualizer component
- Interactive tabbed interface for exploring both cycles

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reveals packaging signals, A-tract bends, and protein binding sites. Features curvature sparklines, flexibility gradients, A-tract detection, and 2D helix trajectory projection. Novelty: 8/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Cool Factor: 8/10.","notes":"Bendability overlay (B): AT proxy per window, sparkline, overlay stack, Esc/B close","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:10:40.824309-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T13:00:47.094209-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T13:00:47.094214-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-qlu","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-2g5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:10:40.825179-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-qm08","content_hash":"7bef6e9b53a8904d62e8635d65e2d2802e2566e073fa47971ef0656d8e9cecb6","title":"Improve Ball-and-Stick CPK element coloring","description":"","notes":"# Improve Ball-and-Stick CPK Element Coloring

## Problem Statement

The current ball-and-stick rendering makes all atoms appear too similar, making it hard to distinguish different elements. While element colors are defined in `structure-loader.ts`, the visual result on dark backgrounds can still look like "a big tangled mass."

## Current State Analysis

**File:** `/packages/web/src/visualization/structure-loader.ts`
**Lines:** 45-59

```typescript
const ELEMENT_COLORS: Record<string, Color> = {
  H: new Color('#e0e0e0'),    // Light gray
  C: new Color('#60a5fa'),    // Bright sky blue (NOT dark gray!)
  N: new Color('#34d399'),    // Bright emerald green
  O: new Color('#f87171'),    // Bright coral red
  S: new Color('#fbbf24'),    // Bright amber
  P: new Color('#c084fc'),    // Purple
  MG: new Color('#4ade80'),   // Bright green
  FE: new Color('#fb923c'),   // Orange
  CA: new Color('#a3e635'),   // Lime
  ZN: new Color('#7dd3fc'),   // Light blue
  CL: new Color('#86efac'),   // Light green
  NA: new Color('#fdba74'),   // Light orange
};
```

## CPK Color Standard

The **CPK coloring scheme** (Corey-Pauling-Koltun) is the industry standard:

| Element | CPK Color | Hex | Rationale |
|---------|-----------|-----|-----------|
| H | White | #FFFFFF | Smallest, most common |
| C | Dark gray/black | #555555 | Carbon backbone |
| N | Blue | #3050F8 | Nitrogen in proteins |
| O | Red | #FF0D0D | Oxygen, polar |
| S | Yellow | #FFFF30 | Sulfur bridges |
| P | Orange | #FF8000 | Phosphate groups |
| Cl | Green | #1FF01F | Halogen |
| Fe | Orange-brown | #E06633 | Iron in heme |
| Ca | Dark green | #3DFF00 | Calcium ions |
| Mg | Dark green | #2A7800 | Magnesium |

## Issues to Address

1. **Carbon too bright:** Currently sky blue, should be darker gray
2. **Low contrast between similar elements:** N and Cl both green-ish
3. **Hydrogen too dark:** Should be brightest (white)
4. **Need sphere scaling by element:** Hydrogen atoms should be smaller

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Update Color Palette to Modified CPK (Dark Theme Optimized)

```typescript
const ELEMENT_COLORS: Record<string, Color> = {
  H: new Color('#f8fafc'),    // Almost white (bright on dark bg)
  C: new Color('#64748b'),    // Slate gray (neutral backbone)
  N: new Color('#3b82f6'),    // True blue (nitrogen)
  O: new Color('#ef4444'),    // True red (oxygen)
  S: new Color('#fde047'),    // Bright yellow (sulfur)
  P: new Color('#fb923c'),    // Orange (phosphorus)
  MG: new Color('#22c55e'),   // Green (magnesium)
  FE: new Color('#ea580c'),   // Rust orange (iron)
  CA: new Color('#16a34a'),   // Dark green (calcium)
  ZN: new Color('#7c3aed'),   // Purple (zinc)
  CL: new Color('#4ade80'),   // Lime green (chlorine)
  NA: new Color('#a855f7'),   // Violet (sodium)
};
```

### 2. Add Element-Based Sphere Scaling

```typescript
const ELEMENT_RADII: Record<string, number> = {
  H: 0.25,   // Smallest
  C: 0.40,   // Medium
  N: 0.38,   // Slightly smaller than C
  O: 0.35,   // Smaller than N
  S: 0.50,   // Larger
  P: 0.52,   // Larger
  // ... etc
};
```

### 3. Improve Material Properties

```typescript
const atomMat = new MeshPhongMaterial({
  vertexColors: true,
  shininess: 100,        // Higher for more reflective spheres
  specular: new Color('#ffffff'),  // White specular for better 3D feel
});
```

## Files to Modify

1. `/packages/web/src/visualization/structure-loader.ts`
   - Update ELEMENT_COLORS
   - Update ELEMENT_RADII
   - Modify buildBallAndStick to use element-specific radii

2. `/packages/web/src/components/Model3DView.tsx`
   - Consider adding a color scheme selector (CPK/RasMol/Custom)

## Testing

- [ ] All common elements have distinct, recognizable colors
- [ ] Works on both light and dark themes
- [ ] Atoms are clearly distinguishable at default zoom
- [ ] Larger structures remain readable

## Definition of Done

- [ ] CPK-derived color scheme implemented
- [ ] Element-based sphere sizing
- [ ] Clear visual distinction between C, N, O, S, P
- [ ] Materials optimized for visibility on dark backgrounds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:47.574575-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:09.001156-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:09.001156-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-qm08","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-vru3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:23.563205-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-qss","content_hash":"02e6e8136e2b5cb40075eb09ad1fa9b560327558a3a7822a1db590876ead50ab","title":"[WEB-P2] Implement getVisibleRange with overscan","description":"","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:58:24.283322-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:01:31.809403-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:01:31.809403-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-qss","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-11b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:05:08.633072-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-qzk","content_hash":"66e5ecb7bdbbbaca2e856f11563da68fb5973323f513e15904b1315335285c14","title":"Advanced: Tail Fiber Tropism & Receptor Atlas","description":"Predict receptor-binding specificity by clustering tail fiber/tip proteins and mapping them to known or candidate host receptors. Sharpens host-range hypotheses and engineering targets.

**Build Plan:**
- Embed tail fibers with ESM/ProtT5 (Python), cluster with HDBSCAN/UMAP
- Optional AlphaFold2-lite batches for 3D hints
- Match against receptor HMMs and host surface proteomes
- Cache clusters and receptor likelihoods
- Inference via ONNX Runtime in Bun for new sequences

**Why It's Good:**
- Connects sequence to receptor-binding lineage and host-range
- Complements codon/tRNA signals with direct molecular evidence
- Engineering targets for host-range expansion/restriction

**Implementation Stack:** Python (ESM/ProtT5) for embeddings, ONNX Runtime for inference, HDBSCAN/UMAP clustering, Rust+WASM for fast matching, SQLite caching.

Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 8/10, Cool Factor: 9/10.","notes":"## TAIL FIBER TROPISM & RECEPTOR ATLAS

**Comprehensive Feature Specification from Roadmap**

### Concept
Predict receptor-binding specificity by clustering tail fiber/tip proteins and mapping them to known or candidate host receptors. Centers on receptor-binding lineage and host-range prediction.

**What This Feature Does:**
- Embeds tail fiber proteins using protein language models (ESM/ProtT5)
- Clusters similar fibers to identify tropism families
- Maps clusters to known receptors (LPS, OmpA, FhuA, etc.)
- Predicts host-range breadth from receptor diversity

### Build Plan

**Step 1: Protein Embedding**
```python
from esm import pretrained
model, alphabet = pretrained.load_model_and_alphabet('esm2_t33_650M_UR50D')
# Embed tail fiber sequences
embeddings = model(tail_fiber_seqs)
```

**Step 2: Clustering**
```python
import hdbscan
import umap

# Reduce dimensions
reducer = umap.UMAP(n_components=2, metric='cosine')
embedding_2d = reducer.fit_transform(embeddings)

# Cluster
clusterer = hdbscan.HDBSCAN(min_cluster_size=5)
labels = clusterer.fit_predict(embedding_2d)
```

**Step 3: Receptor Matching**
- HMM profiles for known receptor-binding domains
- Host surface proteome database
- Confidence scoring: embedding cosine + domain match + literature support

### TUI Visualization

```
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  TAIL FIBER TROPISM & RECEPTOR ATLAS: Lambda Phage                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│  Tail Fiber Cluster: LamB-family (87% confidence)                           │
│                                                                             │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │  TROPISM MAP                                                        │    │
│  │                                                                     │    │
│  │     [LamB]──●──[OmpC]      Current phage: ●                        │    │
│  │        ╲   │   ╱           Cluster members: ○                      │    │
│  │         ╲  │  ╱                                                    │    │
│  │   [FhuA]──○──○──[BtuB]     Edge = receptor overlap                │    │
│  │           │   ╲                                                    │    │
│  │           ○    ╲                                                   │    │
│  │         ╱       [FepA]                                            │    │
│  │      [Tsx]                                                        │    │
│  │                                                                     │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                                                             │
│  Predicted Receptors:                                                       │
│  ├─ 1. LamB (maltoporin)        94% confidence  [E. coli K-12]             │
│  ├─ 2. OmpC                     31% confidence  [Salmonella]               │
│  └─ 3. Unknown porin            12% confidence  [Shigella]                 │
│                                                                             │
│  Host Range Breadth: NARROW (single primary receptor)                       │
│                                                                             │
│  [F] Filter by function  [H] Show host taxa  [C] Cluster details  [ESC]    │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Why It's Good

1. **Direct Molecular Evidence**: Goes beyond sequence similarity to predict actual binding partners
2. **Engineering Targets**: Identifies fiber domains to modify for host-range changes
3. **Complements Other Signals**: Works with codon/tRNA adaptation and CRISPR spacer evidence
4. **Cluster Context**: Shows evolutionary relationships among receptor-binding proteins","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T13:04:27.663149-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:25:18.516407-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-qzk","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ojb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T13:06:05.522106-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

+import { useBeginnerMode } from '../hooks/useBeginnerMode'; +import { InfectionCycleVisualizer } from '../components/InfectionCycleVisualizer'; +import type { ModuleId } from '../types'; + +const MODULE_ID: ModuleId = 'phage-lifecycle'; + +type CycleStep = { + name: string; + description: string; + color: string; + duration?: string; +}; + +type KeyConcept = { + term: string; + definition: string; + example?: string; +}; + +const LYTIC_STEPS: CycleStep[] = [ + { + name: 'Attachment', + description: 'Tail fibers recognize and bind to specific receptors on the bacterial surface.', + color: '#3b82f6', + duration: '< 1 min', + }, + { + name: 'Injection', + description: 'The phage punctures the cell membrane and injects its genome into the host.', + color: '#22c55e', + duration: '~ 1 min', + }, + { + name: 'Takeover', + description: 'Phage genes hijack host machinery, shutting down bacterial functions.', + color: '#f59e0b', + duration: '5-10 min', + }, + { + name: 'Replication', + description: 'Host ribosomes and polymerases produce hundreds of phage copies.', + color: '#8b5cf6', + duration: '10-15 min', + }, + { + name: 'Assembly', + description: 'New capsids form and package replicated genomes into complete virions.', + color: '#ec4899', + duration: '5-10 min', + }, + { + name: 'Lysis', + description: 'Enzymes burst the host cell, releasing 100-200 new phages.', + color: '#ef4444', + duration: '< 1 min', + }, +]; + +const LYSOGENIC_STEPS: CycleStep[] = [ + { + name: 'Attachment & Injection', + description: 'Same initial steps as lytic cycle—genome enters the host cell.', + color: '#3b82f6', + }, + { + name: 'Integration', + description: 'Phage DNA recombines with bacterial chromosome, becoming a prophage.', + color: '#14b8a6', + }, + { + name: 'Dormancy', + description: 'Prophage replicates passively with host DNA through many generations.', + color: '#6b7280', + }, + { + name: 'Induction', + description: 'Stress triggers (UV, SOS response) activate the prophage, switching to lytic.', + color: '#ef4444', + }, +]; + +const KEY_CONCEPTS: KeyConcept[] = [ + { + term: 'Virulent Phage', + definition: 'A phage that only follows the lytic pathway—always kills its host.', + example: 'T4, T7 phages', + }, + { + term: 'Temperate Phage', + definition: 'A phage that can choose between lytic and lysogenic pathways.', + example: 'Lambda (λ), Mu phages', + }, + { + term: 'Prophage', + definition: 'Phage DNA integrated into the host chromosome during lysogeny.', + }, + { + term: 'Lysogen', + definition: 'A bacterial cell carrying a prophage—immune to infection by the same phage.', + }, + { + term: 'Induction', + definition: 'The switch from lysogenic to lytic cycle, triggered by cellular stress.', + }, + { + term: 'Burst Size', + definition: 'Number of new phage particles released per infected cell (typically 50-200).', + }, +]; + +type DecisionFactor = { + factor: string; + lyticBias: string; + lysogenicBias: string; +}; + +const LAMBDA_DECISION_FACTORS: DecisionFactor[] = [ + { + factor: 'MOI (Multiplicity of Infection)', + lyticBias: 'Low MOI (few phages per cell)', + lysogenicBias: 'High MOI (many phages competing)', + }, + { + factor: 'Cell Health', + lyticBias: 'Healthy, rapidly growing cells', + lysogenicBias: 'Starved or stressed cells', + }, + { + factor: 'cI vs Cro Proteins', + lyticBias: 'Cro wins → lytic genes activate', + lysogenicBias: 'cI wins → lysogenic genes activate', + }, +]; Key Takeaways

+
    +
  • + Lytic cycle = rapid, destructive infection ending in cell lysis and phage release. +
    • +
  • + Lysogenic cycle = stealth mode where phage DNA integrates and replicates with the host. +
    • +
  • + Temperate phages can switch between both strategies; virulent phages are lytic-only. +
    • +
  • + Lambda's cI vs Cro circuit is a classic example of a bistable molecular switch. +
    • +
  • + Prophage induction occurs under stress (UV, antibiotics), triggering the lytic pathway. +
